member.name: M:ExpressionToString.ExpressionStringBuilder.ToString(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression,System.Boolean)
member.summary: A nicely formatted ToString of an expression
member.param: The expression to format
If true will replace large (>3) argument lists with an ellipsis

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.CanBeNullAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the value of the marked element could be null sometimes, so checking for null is required before its usage.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the value of the marked element can never be null.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ItemNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Can be applied to symbols of types derived from IEnumerable as well as to symbols of Task and Lazy classes to indicate that the value of a collection item, of the Task.Result property, or of the Lazy.Value property can never be null.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ItemCanBeNullAttribute
member.summary: Can be applied to symbols of types derived from IEnumerable as well as to symbols of Task and Lazy classes to indicate that the value of a collection item, of the Task.Result property, or of the Lazy.Value property can be null.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.StringFormatMethodAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method builds a string by the format pattern and (optional) arguments. The parameter that accepts the format string should be specified in the constructor. The format string should be in the -like form.

member.name: M:JetBrains.Annotations.StringFormatMethodAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.param: Specifies which parameter of an annotated method should be treated as the format string.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.StructuredMessageTemplateAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter is a message template where placeholders are to be replaced by the following arguments in the order in which they appear.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ValueProviderAttribute
member.summary: Use this annotation to specify a type that contains static or const fields with values for the annotated property/field/parameter. The specified type will be used to improve completion suggestions.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ValueRangeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the integral value falls into the specified interval. It is allowed to specify multiple non-intersecting intervals. Values of interval boundaries are included in the interval.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.NonNegativeValueAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the integral value never falls below zero.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.InvokerParameterNameAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the function argument should be a string literal and match one of the parameters of the caller function. This annotation is used for parameters like string paramName parameter of the constructor.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.NotifyPropertyChangedInvocatorAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the method is contained in a type that implements the System.ComponentModel.INotifyPropertyChanged interface and this method is used to notify that some property value changed.
member.remarks:
The method should be non-static and conform to one of the supported signatures:
NotifyChanged(string)
NotifyChanged(params string[])
NotifyChanged{T}(Expression{Func{T}})
NotifyChanged{T,U}(Expression{Func{T,U}})
SetProperty{T}(ref T, T, string)

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ContractAnnotationAttribute
member.summary: Describes dependence between the input and output of a method.
member.syntax:
Function Definition Table syntax:
FDT ::= FDTRow [;FDTRow]*
FDTRow ::= Input => Output | Output <= Input
Input ::= ParameterName: Value [, Input]*
Output ::= [ParameterName: Value]* {halt|stop|void|nothing|Value}
Value ::= true | false | null | notnull | canbenull
If the method has a single input parameter, its name could be omitted.
Using halt (or void/nothing, which is the same) for the method output means that the method doesn't return normally (throws or terminates the process).
The canbenull value is only applicable to output parameters.
You can use multiple [ContractAnnotation] for each FDT row, or use a single attribute with rows separated by the semicolon. The order of rows doesn't matter, all rows are checked for applicability and applied per each program state tracked by the analysis engine.

item.code: [ContractAnnotation("null <= param:null")] // reverse condition syntax public string GetName(string surname)
[ContractAnnotation("s:null => true")] public bool IsNullOrEmpty(string s) // string.IsNullOrEmpty()
[ContractAnnotation("=> true, result: notnull; => false, result: null")] public bool TryParse(string s, out Person result)

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.LocalizationRequiredAttribute
member.summary: Indicates whether the marked element should be localized.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.CannotApplyEqualityOperatorAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the value of the marked type (or its derivatives) cannot be compared using == or != operators, and Equals() should be used instead. However, using == or != for comparison with null is always permitted.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.DefaultEqualityUsageAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the method or type uses equality members of the annotated element.
member.remarks: When applied to the method's generic parameter, indicates that the equality of the annotated type is used, unless a custom equality comparer is passed when calling this method. The attribute can also be applied directly to the method's parameter or return type to specify equality usage for it. When applied to the type's generic parameter, indicates that type equality usage can happen anywhere inside this type, so the instantiation of this type is treated as equality usage, unless a custom equality comparer is passed to the constructor.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.BaseTypeRequiredAttribute
member.summary: When applied to a target attribute, specifies a requirement for any type marked with the target attribute to implement or inherit the specific type or types.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.UsedImplicitlyAttribute
member.summary:
Indicates that the marked symbol is used implicitly (via reflection, in an external library, and so on), so this symbol will be ignored by usage-checking inspections.
You can use and to configure how this attribute is applied.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.MeansImplicitUseAttribute
member.summary: Can be applied to attributes, type parameters, and parameters of a type assignable from . When applied to an attribute, the decorated attribute behaves the same as . When applied to a type parameter or to a parameter of type , indicates that the corresponding type is used implicitly.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseKindFlags
member.summary: Specifies the details of an implicitly used symbol when it is marked with or .

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseKindFlags.Access
member.summary: Only entity marked with attribute considered used.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseKindFlags.Assign
member.summary: Indicates implicit assignment to a member.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseKindFlags.InstantiatedWithFixedConstructorSignature
member.summary: Indicates implicit instantiation of a type with fixed constructor signature. That means any unused constructor parameters will not be reported as such.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseKindFlags.InstantiatedNoFixedConstructorSignature
member.summary: Indicates implicit instantiation of a type.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseTargetFlags
member.summary: Specifies what is considered to be used implicitly when marked with or .

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseTargetFlags.Members
member.summary: Members of the type marked with the attribute are considered used.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseTargetFlags.WithInheritors
member.summary: Inherited entities are considered used.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.ImplicitUseTargetFlags.WithMembers
member.summary: Entity marked with the attribute and all its members considered used.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.PublicAPIAttribute
member.summary: This attribute is intended to mark publicly available APIs that should not be removed and therefore should never be reported as unused.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.InstantHandleAttribute
member.summary: Tells the code analysis engine if the parameter is completely handled when the invoked method is on stack. If the parameter is a delegate, indicates that the delegate can only be invoked during method execution (the delegate can be invoked zero or multiple times, but not stored to some field and invoked later, when the containing method is no longer on the execution stack). If the parameter is an enumerable, indicates that it is enumerated while the method is executed. If is true, the attribute will only take effect if the method invocation is located under the await expression.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.InstantHandleAttribute.RequireAwait
member.summary: Requires the method invocation to be used under the await expression for this attribute to take effect. Can be used for delegate/enumerable parameters of async methods.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.PureAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the method does not make any observable state changes. The same as .

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.MustUseReturnValueAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the return value of the method invocation must be used.
member.remarks:
Methods decorated with this attribute (in contrast to pure methods) might change state, but make no sense without using their return value.
Similarly to , this attribute will help detect usages of the method when the return value is not used. Optionally, you can specify a message to use when showing warnings, e.g. [MustUseReturnValue("Use the return value to...")].

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.MustUseReturnValueAttribute.IsFluentBuilderMethod
member.summary: Enables the special handling of the "fluent" APIs that perform mutations and return 'this' object. In this case the analysis checks the fluent invocations chain and only warns if the initial receiver value is probably a temporary value - in this case the very last fluent method return assumed to be temporary as well, therefore is a subject of warning if unused. If the initial receiver is a local variable or 'this' reference the analysis assumes that fluent invocations were used to mutate the existing value and warning will not be shown.
member.remarks: This property must only be used for methods with the return type matching the receiver type.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.MustDisposeResourceAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the resource disposal must be handled at the use site, meaning that the resource ownership is transferred to the caller. This annotation can be used to annotate disposable types or their constructors individually to enable the IDE code analysis for resource disposal in every context where the new instance of this type is created. Factory methods and out parameters can also be annotated to indicate that the return value of the disposable type needs handling.
member.remarks:
Annotation of input parameters with this attribute is meaningless.
Constructors inherit this attribute from their type if it is annotated, but not from the base constructors they delegate to (if any).
Resource disposal is expected via using (resource) statement, using var declaration, explicit Dispose() call, or passing the resource as an argument to a parameter annotated with the attribute.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.MustDisposeResourceAttribute.Value
member.summary: When set to false, disposing of the resource is not obligatory. The main use-case for explicit [MustDisposeResource(false)] annotation is to loosen the annotation for inheritors.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.HandlesResourceDisposalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that method or class instance acquires resource ownership and will dispose it after use.
member.remarks:
Annotation of out parameters with this attribute is meaningless.
When an instance method is annotated with this attribute, it means that it is handling the resource disposal of the corresponding resource instance.
When a field or a property is annotated with this attribute, it means that this type owns the resource and will handle the resource disposal properly (e.g. in own IDisposable implementation).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.RequireStaticDelegateAttribute
member.summary: This annotation allows enforcing allocation-less usage patterns of delegates for performance-critical APIs. When this annotation is applied to the parameter of a delegate type, the IDE checks the input argument of this parameter: * When a lambda expression or anonymous method is passed as an argument, the IDE verifies that the passed closure has no captures of the containing local variables and the compiler is able to cache the delegate instance to avoid heap allocations. Otherwise, a warning is produced. * The IDE warns when the method name or local function name is passed as an argument because this always results in heap allocation of the delegate instance.
member.remarks: In C# 9.0+ code, the IDE will also suggest annotating the anonymous functions with the static modifier to make use of the similar analysis provided by the language/compiler.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.ProvidesContextAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the type member or parameter of some type that should be used instead of all other ways to get the value of that type. This annotation is useful when you have some 'context' value evaluated and stored somewhere, meaning that all other ways to get this value must be consolidated with the existing one.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.PathReferenceAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that a parameter is a path to a file or a folder within a web project. Path can be relative or absolute, starting from web root (~).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.SourceTemplateAttribute
member.summary: An extension method marked with this attribute is processed by code completion as a 'Source Template'. When the extension method is completed over some expression, its source code is automatically expanded like a template at the call site.
member.remarks: Template method bodies can contain valid source code and/or special comments starting with '$'. Text inside these comments is added as source code when the template is applied. Template parameters can be used either as additional method parameters or as identifiers wrapped in two '$' signs. Use the attribute to specify macros for parameters. The expression to be used in the expansion can be adjusted by the parameter.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.SourceTemplateAttribute.Target
member.summary: Allows specifying the expression to capture for template execution if more than one expression is available at the expansion point. If not specified, is assumed.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.SourceTemplateTargetExpression
member.summary: Provides a value for the to define how to capture the expression at the point of expansion

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.SourceTemplateTargetExpression.Inner
member.summary: Selects inner expression

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.SourceTemplateTargetExpression.Outer
member.summary: Selects outer expression

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.MacroAttribute
member.summary: Allows specifying a macro for a parameter of a source template.
member.remarks: You can apply the attribute to the whole method or to any of its additional parameters. The macro expression is defined in the property. When applied to a method, the target template parameter is defined in the property. To apply the macro silently for the parameter, set the property value to -1.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.MacroAttribute.Expression
member.summary: Allows specifying a macro that will be executed for a source template parameter when the template is expanded.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.MacroAttribute.Editable
member.summary: Allows specifying the occurrence of the target parameter that becomes editable when the template is deployed.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.MacroAttribute.Target
member.summary: Identifies the target parameter of a source template if the is applied to a template method.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.CollectionAccessAttribute
member.summary: Indicates how a method, constructor invocation, or property access over a collection type affects the contents of the collection. When applied to a return value of a method, indicates whether the returned collection is created exclusively for the caller (CollectionAccessType.UpdatedContent) or can be read/updated from outside (CollectionAccessType.Read/CollectionAccessType.UpdatedContent). Use to specify the access type.
member.remarks: Using this attribute only makes sense if all collection methods are marked with this attribute.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.CollectionAccessType
member.summary: Provides a value for the to define how the collection method invocation affects the contents of the collection.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.CollectionAccessType.None
member.summary: Method does not use or modify content of the collection.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.CollectionAccessType.Read
member.summary: Method only reads content of the collection but does not modify it.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.CollectionAccessType.ModifyExistingContent
member.summary: Method can change content of the collection but does not add new elements.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.CollectionAccessType.UpdatedContent
member.summary: Method can add new elements to the collection.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionMethodAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method is an assertion method, i.e. it halts the control flow if one of the conditions is satisfied. To set the condition, mark one of the parameters with attribute.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionConditionAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the condition parameter of the assertion method. The method itself should be marked by the attribute. The mandatory argument of the attribute is the assertion type.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionConditionType
member.summary: Specifies the assertion type. If the assertion method argument satisfies the condition, then the execution continues. Otherwise, execution is assumed to be halted.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionConditionType.IS_TRUE
member.summary: Marked parameter should be evaluated to true.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionConditionType.IS_FALSE
member.summary: Marked parameter should be evaluated to false.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionConditionType.IS_NULL
member.summary: Marked parameter should be evaluated to null value.

member.name: F:JetBrains.Annotations.AssertionConditionType.IS_NOT_NULL
member.summary: Marked parameter should be evaluated to not null value.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.TerminatesProgramAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method unconditionally terminates control flow execution. For example, it could unconditionally throw an exception.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.LinqTunnelAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the method is a pure LINQ method with postponed enumeration (like Enumerable.Select or Enumerable.Where). This annotation allows inference of the [InstantHandle] annotation for parameters of delegate type by analyzing LINQ method chains.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.NoEnumerationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an IEnumerable passed as a parameter is not enumerated. Use this annotation to suppress the 'Possible multiple enumeration of IEnumerable' inspection.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.RegexPatternAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter, field, or property is a regular expression pattern.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.InjectedLanguage
member.summary: Language of the injected code fragment inside a string literal marked by the .

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.LanguageInjectionAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter, field, or property accepts string literals containing code fragments in a specified language.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.LanguageInjectionAttribute.InjectedLanguage
member.summary: Specifies a language of the injected code fragment.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.LanguageInjectionAttribute.InjectedLanguageName
member.summary: Specifies a language name of the injected code fragment.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.LanguageInjectionAttribute.Prefix
member.summary: Specifies a string that 'precedes' the injected string literal.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.LanguageInjectionAttribute.Suffix
member.summary: Specifies a string that 'follows' the injected string literal.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.NoReorderAttribute
member.summary: Prevents the Member Reordering feature in the IDE from tossing members of the marked class.
member.remarks: The attribute must be mentioned in your member reordering patterns.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute
member.summary: Defines a code search pattern using the Structural Search and Replace syntax. It allows you to find and, if necessary, replace blocks of code that match a specific pattern.
member.remarks:
Search and replace patterns consist of a textual part and placeholders. Textural part must contain only identifiers allowed in the target language and will be matched exactly (whitespaces, tabulation characters, and line breaks are ignored). Placeholders allow matching variable parts of the target code blocks.
A placeholder has the following format: $placeholder_name$ - where placeholder_name is an arbitrary identifier. Predefined placeholders:
$this$ - expression of containing type
$thisType$ - containing type
$member$ - current member placeholder
$qualifier$ - this placeholder is available in the replace pattern and can be used to insert a qualifier expression matched by the $member$ placeholder. (Note that if $qualifier$ placeholder is used, then $member$ placeholder will match only qualified references)
$expression$ - expression of any type
$identifier$ - identifier placeholder
$args$ - any number of arguments
$arg$ - single argument
$arg1$ ... $arg10$ - single argument
$stmts$ - any number of statements
$stmt$ - single statement
$stmt1$ ... $stmt10$ - single statement
$name{Expression, 'Namespace.FooType'}$ - expression with the Namespace.FooType type
$expression{'Namespace.FooType'}$ - expression with the Namespace.FooType type
$name{Type, 'Namespace.FooType'}$ - Namespace.FooType type
$type{'Namespace.FooType'}$ - Namespace.FooType type
$statement{1,2}$ - 1 or 2 statements
You can also define your own placeholders of the supported types and specify arguments for each placeholder type. This can be done using the following format: $name{type, arguments}$. Where name - is the name of your placeholder, type - is the type of your placeholder (one of the following: Expression, Type, Identifier, Statement, Argument, Member), arguments - a list of arguments for your placeholder. Each placeholder type supports its own arguments. Check the examples below for more details. The placeholder type may be omitted and determined from the placeholder name, if the name has one of the following prefixes:
expr, expression - expression placeholder, e.g. $exprPlaceholder{}$, $expressionFoo{}$
arg, argument - argument placeholder, e.g. $argPlaceholder{}$, $argumentFoo{}$
ident, identifier - identifier placeholder, e.g. $identPlaceholder{}$, $identifierFoo{}$
stmt, statement - statement placeholder, e.g. $stmtPlaceholder{}$, $statementFoo{}$
type - type placeholder, e.g. $typePlaceholder{}$, $typeFoo{}$
member - member placeholder, e.g. $memberPlaceholder{}$, $memberFoo{}$
member.para:
Expression placeholder arguments:
expressionType - string value in single quotes, specifies full type name to match (empty string by default)
exactType - boolean value, specifies if expression should have exact type match (false by default)
Examples:
$myExpr{Expression, 'Namespace.FooType', true}$ - defines an expression placeholder matching expressions of the Namespace.FooType type with exact matching.
$myExpr{Expression, 'Namespace.FooType'}$ - defines an expression placeholder matching expressions of the Namespace.FooType type or expressions that can be implicitly converted to Namespace.FooType.
$myExpr{Expression}$ - defines an expression placeholder matching expressions of any type.
$exprFoo{'Namespace.FooType', true}$ - defines an expression placeholder matching expressions of the Namespace.FooType type with exact matching.

Type placeholder arguments:
type - string value in single quotes, specifies the full type name to match (empty string by default)
exactType - boolean value, specifies whether the expression should have the exact type match (false by default)
Examples:
$myType{Type, 'Namespace.FooType', true}$ - defines a type placeholder matching Namespace.FooType types with exact matching.
$myType{Type, 'Namespace.FooType'}$ - defines a type placeholder matching Namespace.FooType types or types that can be implicitly converted to Namespace.FooType.
$myType{Type}$ - defines a type placeholder matching any type.
$typeFoo{'Namespace.FooType', true}$ - defines a type placeholder matching Namespace.FooType types with exact matching.

Identifier placeholder arguments:
nameRegex - string value in single quotes, specifies regex to use for matching (empty string by default)
nameRegexCaseSensitive - boolean value, specifies if name regex is case-sensitive (true by default)
type - string value in single quotes, specifies full type name to match (empty string by default)
exactType - boolean value, specifies if expression should have exact type match (false by default)
Examples:
$myIdentifier{Identifier, 'my.*', false, 'Namespace.FooType', true}$ - defines an identifier placeholder matching identifiers (ignoring case) starting with my prefix with Namespace.FooType type.
$myIdentifier{Identifier, 'my.*', true, 'Namespace.FooType', true}$ - defines an identifier placeholder matching identifiers (case sensitively) starting with my prefix with Namespace.FooType type.
$identFoo{'my.*'}$ - defines an identifier placeholder matching identifiers (case sensitively) starting with my prefix.

Statement placeholder arguments:
minimalOccurrences - minimal number of statements to match (-1 by default)
maximalOccurrences - maximal number of statements to match (-1 by default)
Examples:
$myStmt{Statement, 1, 2}$ - defines a statement placeholder matching 1 or 2 statements.
$myStmt{Statement}$ - defines a statement placeholder matching any number of statements.
$stmtFoo{1, 2}$ - defines a statement placeholder matching 1 or 2 statements.

Argument placeholder arguments:
minimalOccurrences - minimal number of arguments to match (-1 by default)
maximalOccurrences - maximal number of arguments to match (-1 by default)
Examples:
$myArg{Argument, 1, 2}$ - defines an argument placeholder matching 1 or 2 arguments.
$myArg{Argument}$ - defines an argument placeholder matching any number of arguments.
$argFoo{1, 2}$ - defines an argument placeholder matching 1 or 2 arguments.

Member placeholder arguments:
docId - string value in single quotes, specifies XML documentation ID of the member to match (empty by default)
Examples:
$myMember{Member, 'M:System.String.IsNullOrEmpty(System.String)'}$ - defines a member placeholder matching IsNullOrEmpty member of the System.String type.
$memberFoo{'M:System.String.IsNullOrEmpty(System.String)'}$ - defines a member placeholder matching IsNullOrEmpty member of the System.String type.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.SearchTemplate
member.summary: Structural search pattern.
member.remarks: The pattern includes a textual part, which must only contain identifiers allowed in the target language and placeholders to match variable parts of the target code blocks.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.Message
member.summary: Message to show when a code block matching the search pattern was found.
member.remarks: You can also prepend the message text with 'Error:', 'Warning:', 'Suggestion:' or 'Hint:' prefix to specify the pattern severity. Code patterns with replace templates have the 'Suggestion' severity by default. If a replace pattern is not provided, the pattern will have the 'Warning' severity.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.ReplaceTemplate
member.summary: Replace pattern to use for replacing a matched pattern.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.ReplaceMessage
member.summary: Replace message to show in the light bulb.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.FormatAfterReplace
member.summary: Apply code formatting after code replacement.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.MatchSimilarConstructs
member.summary: Whether similar code blocks should be matched.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.ShortenReferences
member.summary: Automatically insert namespace import directives or remove qualifiers that become redundant after the template is applied.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.CodeTemplateAttribute.SuppressionKey
member.summary: The string to use as a suppression key. By default, the following suppression key is used: CodeTemplate_SomeType_SomeMember, where 'SomeType' and 'SomeMember' are names of the associated containing type and member, to which this attribute is applied.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.IgnoreSpellingAndGrammarErrorsAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the string literal passed as an argument to this parameter should not be checked for spelling or grammar errors.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcActionAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. If applied to a parameter, indicates that the parameter is an MVC action. If applied to a method, the MVC action name is calculated implicitly from the context. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Html.ChildActionExtensions.RenderAction(HtmlHelper, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcAreaAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Indicates that the marked parameter is an MVC area. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Html.ChildActionExtensions.RenderAction(HtmlHelper, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcControllerAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. If applied to a parameter, indicates that the parameter is an MVC controller. If applied to a method, the MVC controller name is calculated implicitly from the context. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Html.ChildActionExtensions.RenderAction(HtmlHelper, String, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcMasterAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Indicates that the marked parameter is an MVC Master. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Controller.View(String, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcModelTypeAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Indicates that the marked parameter is an MVC model type. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Controller.View(String, Object).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcPartialViewAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. If applied to a parameter, indicates that the parameter is an MVC partial view. If applied to a method, the MVC partial view name is calculated implicitly from the context. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Html.RenderPartialExtensions.RenderPartial(HtmlHelper, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcSuppressViewErrorAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Allows disabling inspections for MVC views within a class or a method.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcDisplayTemplateAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Indicates that a parameter is an MVC display template. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Html.DisplayExtensions.DisplayForModel(HtmlHelper, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcEditorTemplateAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Indicates that the marked parameter is an MVC editor template. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Html.EditorExtensions.EditorForModel(HtmlHelper, String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcTemplateAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. Indicates that the marked parameter is an MVC template. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations.UIHintAttribute(System.String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcViewAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. If applied to a parameter, indicates that the parameter is an MVC view component. If applied to a method, the MVC view name is calculated implicitly from the context. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.Mvc.Controller.View(Object).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcViewComponentAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. If applied to a parameter, indicates that the parameter is an MVC view component name.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcViewComponentViewAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. If applied to a parameter, indicates that the parameter is an MVC view component view. If applied to a method, the MVC view component view name is default.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMvcActionSelectorAttribute
member.summary: ASP.NET MVC attribute. When applied to a parameter of an attribute, indicates that this parameter is an MVC action name.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.RouteTemplateAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter, field, or property is a route template.
member.remarks: This attribute allows IDE to recognize the use of web frameworks' route templates to enable syntax highlighting, code completion, navigation, rename and other features in string literals.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.RouteParameterConstraintAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked type is custom route parameter constraint, which is registered in the application's Startup with the name ConstraintName.
member.remarks: You can specify ProposedType if target constraint matches only route parameters of specific type, it will allow IDE to create method's parameter from usage in route template with specified type instead of default System.String and check if constraint's proposed type conflicts with matched parameter's type.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.UriStringAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter, field, or property is a URI string.
member.remarks: This attribute enables code completion, navigation, renaming, and other features in URI string literals assigned to annotated parameters, fields, or properties.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspRouteConventionAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method declares routing convention for ASP.NET.
member.remarks: The IDE will analyze all usages of methods marked with this attribute, and will add all routes to completion, navigation, and other features over URI strings.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspDefaultRouteValuesAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method parameter contains default route values of routing convention for ASP.NET.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspRouteValuesConstraintsAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method parameter contains constraints on route values of routing convention for ASP.NET.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspRouteOrderAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter or property contains routing order provided by ASP.NET routing attribute.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspRouteVerbsAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter or property contains HTTP verbs provided by ASP.NET routing attribute.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspAttributeRoutingAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked attribute is used for attribute routing in ASP.NET.
member.remarks: The IDE will analyze all usages of attributes marked with this attribute, and will add all routes to completion, navigation and other features over URI strings.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMinimalApiDeclarationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method declares an ASP.NET Minimal API endpoint.
member.remarks: The IDE will analyze all usages of methods marked with this attribute, and will add all routes to completion, navigation and other features over URI strings.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMinimalApiGroupAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method declares an ASP.NET Minimal API endpoints group.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMinimalApiHandlerAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked parameter contains an ASP.NET Minimal API endpoint handler.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMinimalApiImplicitEndpointDeclarationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method contains Minimal API endpoint declaration.
member.remarks: The IDE will analyze all usages of methods marked with this attribute, and will add all declared in attributes routes to completion, navigation and other features over URI strings.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.AspMinimalApiImplicitEndpointDeclarationAttribute.QueryParameters
member.summary: Comma-separated list of query parameters defined for endpoint

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.RazorSectionAttribute
member.summary: Razor attribute. Indicates that the marked parameter or method is a Razor section. Use this attribute for custom wrappers similar to System.Web.WebPages.WebPageBase.RenderSection(String).

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.XamlItemsControlAttribute
member.summary: XAML attribute. Indicates the type that has an ItemsSource property and should be treated as an ItemsControl-derived type, to enable inner items DataContext type resolution.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.XamlItemBindingOfItemsControlAttribute
member.summary: XAML attribute. Indicates the property of some BindingBase-derived type, that is used to bind some item of an ItemsControl-derived type. This annotation will enable the DataContext type resolution for XAML bindings for such properties.
member.remarks: The property should have a tree ancestor of the ItemsControl type or marked with the attribute.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.XamlItemStyleOfItemsControlAttribute
member.summary: XAML attribute. Indicates the property of some Style-derived type that is used to style items of an ItemsControl-derived type. This annotation will enable the DataContext type resolution in XAML bindings for such properties.
member.remarks: Property should have a tree ancestor of the ItemsControl type or marked with the attribute.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.XamlOneWayBindingModeByDefaultAttribute
member.summary: XAML attribute. Indicates that DependencyProperty has OneWay binding mode by default.
member.remarks: This attribute must be applied to DependencyProperty's CLR accessor property if it is present, or to a DependencyProperty descriptor field otherwise.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.XamlTwoWayBindingModeByDefaultAttribute
member.summary: XAML attribute. Indicates that DependencyProperty has TwoWay binding mode by default.
member.remarks: This attribute must be applied to DependencyProperty's CLR accessor property if it is present, or to a DependencyProperty descriptor field otherwise.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.TestSubjectAttribute
member.summary: Specifies a type being tested by a test class or a test method.
member.remarks: This information can be used by the IDE to navigate between tests and tested types, or by test runners to group tests by subject and to provide better test reports.

member.name: P:JetBrains.Annotations.TestSubjectAttribute.Subject
member.summary: Gets the type being tested.

member.name: M:JetBrains.Annotations.TestSubjectAttribute.#ctor(System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified tested type.
member.param: The type being tested.

member.name: T:JetBrains.Annotations.MeansTestSubjectAttribute
member.summary: Marks a generic argument as the test subject for a test class.
member.remarks: Can be applied to a generic parameter of a base test class to indicate that the type passed as the argument is the class being tested. This information can be used by the IDE to navigate between tests and tested types, or by test runners to group tests by subject and to provide better test reports.

member.name: M:Shouldly.CallContext.LogicalSetData(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Stores a given object and associates it with the specified name.
member.param: The name with which to associate the new item in the call context.
The object to store in the call context.

member.name: M:Shouldly.CallContext.LogicalGetData(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves an object with the specified name from the .
member.param: The name of the item in the call context.
member.returns: The object in the call context associated with the specified name, or if not found.

member.name: P:Shouldly.Configuration.FirstNonShouldlyMethodFinder.Offset
member.summary: Increasing the offset will move past the first non-shouldly method Anonymous methods are not counted in the offset. This is useful when you have created a reusable method which is calling ShouldMatchApproved

member.name: P:Shouldly.Configuration.ShouldMatchConfiguration.FileExtension
member.summary: File extension without the.

member.name: P:Shouldly.Configuration.ShouldMatchConfiguration.Scrubber
member.summary:
Scrubbers allow you to alter the received document before comparing it to approved.
This is useful for replacing dates or dynamic data with fixed data

member.name: M:Shouldly.Configuration.ShouldMatchConfigurationBuilder.DoNotIgnoreLineEndings
member.summary: Default is to ignore line endings

member.name: M:Shouldly.Configuration.ShouldMatchConfigurationBuilder.SubFolder(System.String)
member.summary: Places the .approved and .received files into a subfolder

member.name: M:Shouldly.Configuration.ShouldMatchConfigurationBuilder.UseCallerLocation
member.summary: Tells shouldly to use this methods caller for naming. Useful when you have created a test helper

member.name: M:Shouldly.Configuration.ShouldMatchConfigurationBuilder.LocateTestMethodUsingAttribute``1
member.summary: Tells shouldly to use this methods caller for naming. Useful when you have created a test helper

member.name: M:Shouldly.DifferenceHighlighting.DifferenceHighlighter.HighlightDifferences(Shouldly.IShouldlyAssertionContext)
member.summary: Compares an actual value against an expected one and creates a string with the differences highlighted

member.name: T:Shouldly.DifferenceHighlighting.EnumerableDifferenceHighlighter
member.summary: Highlights differences between IEnumerables of the same type, marking differences with asterisks

member.name: T:Shouldly.DifferenceHighlighting.IDifferenceHighlighter
member.summary: Interface for classes which can highlight differences in things

member.name: T:Shouldly.Internals.XunitV3Markers.IAssertionException
member.summary: This is a marker interface to allow xUnit.net v3 to detect a test has failed caused by an assertion.

member.name: T:Shouldly.Internals.XunitV3Markers.ITestTimeoutException
member.summary: This is a marker interface to allow xUnit.net v3 to detect a test has failed caused by a timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldlyCoreExtensions.IsSystemDynamicMachinery(System.Reflection.MethodBase)
member.summary: Required to support the method that takes in a as a parameter. Having a method that takes a dynamic really stuffs up the stack trace because the runtime binder has to inject a whole heap of methods. Our normal way of just taking the next frame doesn't work. The following two lines seem to work for now, but this feels like a hack. The conditions to be able to walk up stack trace until we get to the calling method might have to be updated regularly as we find more scenarios. Alternately, it could be replaced with a more robust implementation.

member.name: T:Shouldly.ShouldlyMethodsAttribute
member.summary: Should be used on any class which contains shouldly methods

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldThrowAssertionContext.#ctor(System.Object,System.Object,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Diagnostics.StackTrace,System.String)
member.summary: Only pass stacktrace if asynchronous

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldlyConfiguration.DisableSourceInErrors
member.summary: When set to true shouldly will not try and create better error messages using your source code

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeTestExtensions.ShouldBePositive(System.Decimal,System.String)
member.summary: decimal

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeTestExtensions.ShouldBePositive(System.Double,System.String)
member.summary: double

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeTestExtensions.ShouldBePositive(System.Single,System.String)
member.summary: float

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeTestExtensions.ShouldBePositive(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: int

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeTestExtensions.ShouldBePositive(System.Int64,System.String)
member.summary: long

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeTestExtensions.ShouldBePositive(System.Int16,System.String)
member.summary: short

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeStringTestExtensions.ShouldContainWithoutWhitespace(System.String,System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Strip out whitespace (whitespace, tabs, line-endings, etc) and compare the two strings

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeStringTestExtensions.ShouldBe(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Perform a string comparison with sensitivity options

member.name: M:Shouldly.ShouldBeStringTestExtensions.ShouldBe(System.String,System.String,Shouldly.StringCompareShould)
member.summary: Perform a string comparison with sensitivity options

member.name: T:Shouldly.AsyncShouldlyThrowShouldlyMessage
member.summary: Async methods need stacktrace before we get asynchronous

member.name: T:Shouldly.AsyncShouldlyNotThrowShouldlyMessage
member.summary: Async methods need stacktrace before we get asynchronous

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.CompleteIn(System.Action,System.TimeSpan,System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that the given action completes within the specified timeout.
member.param: The action to execute.
The maximum time allowed for the action to complete.
Optional custom message to use if the assertion fails.
member.exception: Thrown when the action does not complete within the specified timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.CompleteIn``1(System.Func{``0},System.TimeSpan,System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that the given function completes within the specified timeout and returns its result.
member.typeparam: The type of the result returned by the function.
member.param: The function to execute.
The maximum time allowed for the function to complete.
Optional custom message to use if the assertion fails.
member.returns: The result of the function if it completes within the timeout.
member.exception: Thrown when the function does not complete within the specified timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.CompleteIn(System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.TimeSpan,System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that the given asynchronous function completes within the specified timeout.
member.param: The asynchronous function to execute.
The maximum time allowed for the function to complete.
Optional custom message to use if the assertion fails.
member.exception: Thrown when the function does not complete within the specified timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.CompleteIn``1(System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.TimeSpan,System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that the given asynchronous function completes within the specified timeout and returns its result.
member.typeparam: The type of the result returned by the asynchronous function.
member.param: The asynchronous function to execute.
The maximum time allowed for the function to complete.
Optional custom message to use if the assertion fails.
member.returns: The result of the asynchronous function if it completes within the timeout.
member.exception: Thrown when the function does not complete within the specified timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.CompleteIn(System.Threading.Tasks.Task,System.TimeSpan,System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that the given task completes within the specified timeout.
member.param: The task to wait for completion.
The maximum time allowed for the task to complete.
Optional custom message to use if the assertion fails.
member.exception: Thrown when the task does not complete within the specified timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.CompleteIn``1(System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0},System.TimeSpan,System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that the given task completes within the specified timeout and returns its result.
member.typeparam: The type of the result returned by the task.
member.param: The task to wait for completion.
The maximum time allowed for the task to complete.
Optional custom message to use if the assertion fails.
member.returns: The result of the task if it completes within the timeout.
member.exception: Thrown when the task does not complete within the specified timeout.

member.name: M:Shouldly.Should.NotThrowInternal``1(System.Func{``0},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Used to differentiate between the extension methods and the static methods

member.name: T:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics
member.summary: Helper routines for working with floating point numbers

remarks.para: The floating point comparison code is based on this excellent article: http://www.cygnus-software.com/papers/comparingfloats/comparingfloats.htm

"ULP" means Unit in the Last Place and in the context of this library refers to the distance between two adjacent floating point numbers. IEEE floating point numbers can only represent a finite subset of natural numbers, with greater accuracy for smaller numbers and lower accuracy for very large numbers.

If a comparison is allowed "2 ulps" of deviation, that means the values are allowed to deviate by up to 2 adjacent floating point values, which might be as low as 0.0000001 for small numbers or as high as 10.0 for large numbers.

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.AreAlmostEqualUlps(System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Compares two floating point values for equality
member.param: First floating point value to be compared
Second floating point value t be compared
Maximum number of representable floating point values that are allowed to be between the left and the right floating point values
member.returns: True if both numbers are equal or close to being equal

remarks.para: Floating point values can only represent a finite subset of natural numbers. For example, the values 2.00000000 and 2.00000024 can be stored in a float, but nothing inbetween them.

This comparison will count how many possible floating point values are between the left and the right number. If the number of possible values between both numbers is less than or equal to maxUlps, then the numbers are considered as being equal.
Implementation partially follows the code outlined here: http://www.anttirt.net/2007/08/19/proper-floating-point-comparisons/

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.AreAlmostEqualUlps(System.Double,System.Double,System.Int64)
member.summary: Compares two double precision floating point values for equality
member.param: First double precision floating point value to be compared
Second double precision floating point value t be compared
Maximum number of representable double precision floating point values that are allowed to be between the left and the right double precision floating point values
member.returns: True if both numbers are equal or close to being equal

remarks.para: Double precision floating point values can only represent a limited series of natural numbers. For example, the values 2.0000000000000000 and 2.0000000000000004 can be stored in a double, but nothing inbetween them.

This comparison will count how many possible double precision floating point values are between the left and the right number. If the number of possible values between both numbers is less than or equal to maxUlps, then the numbers are considered as being equal.
Implementation partially follows the code outlined here: http://www.anttirt.net/2007/08/19/proper-floating-point-comparisons/

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.ReinterpretAsInt(System.Single)
member.summary: Reinterprets the memory contents of a floating point value as an integer value
member.param: Floating point value whose memory contents to reinterpret
member.returns: The memory contents of the floating point value interpreted as an integer

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.ReinterpretAsLong(System.Double)
member.summary: Reinterprets the memory contents of a double precision floating point value as an integer value
member.param: Double precision floating point value whose memory contents to reinterpret
member.returns: The memory contents of the double precision floating point value interpreted as an integer

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.ReinterpretAsFloat(System.Int32)
member.summary: Reinterprets the memory contents of an integer as a floating point value
member.param: Integer value whose memory contents to reinterpret
member.returns: The memory contents of the integer value interpreted as a floating point value

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.ReinterpretAsDouble(System.Int64)
member.summary: Reinterprets the memory contents of an integer value as a double precision floating point value
member.param: Integer whose memory contents to reinterpret
member.returns: The memory contents of the integer interpreted as a double precision floating point value

member.name: T:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.DoubleLongUnion
member.summary: Union of a double precision floating point variable and a long

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.DoubleLongUnion.Double
member.summary: The union's value as a double precision floating point variable

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.DoubleLongUnion.Long
member.summary: The union's value as a long

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.DoubleLongUnion.ULong
member.summary: The union's value as an unsigned long

member.name: T:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.FloatIntUnion
member.summary: Union of a floating point variable and an integer

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.FloatIntUnion.Float
member.summary: The union's value as a floating point variable

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.FloatIntUnion.Int
member.summary: The union's value as an integer

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.FloatingPointNumerics.FloatIntUnion.UInt
member.summary: The union's value as an unsigned integer

member.name: T:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Numerics
member.summary: The Numerics class contains common operations on numeric values.

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Numerics.IsNumericType(System.Object)
member.summary: Checks the type of the object, returning true if the object is a numeric type.
member.param: The object to check
member.returns: true if the object is a numeric type

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Numerics.AreEqual(System.Object,System.Object,NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance@)
member.summary: Test two numeric values for equality, performing the usual numeric conversions and using a provided or default tolerance. If the tolerance provided is Empty, this method may set it to a default tolerance.
member.param: The expected value
The actual value
A reference to the tolerance in effect
member.returns: True if the values are equal

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Numerics.Compare(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Compare two numeric values, performing the usual numeric conversions.
member.param: The expected value
The actual value
member.returns: The relationship of the values to each other

member.name: T:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance
member.summary: The Tolerance class generalizes the notion of a tolerance within which an equality test succeeds. Normally, it is used with numeric types, but it can be used with any type that supports taking a difference between two objects and comparing that difference to a value.

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.#ctor(System.Object)
member.summary: Constructs a linear tolerance of a specified amount

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.#ctor(System.Object,NUnit.Framework.Constraints.ToleranceMode)
member.summary: Constructs a tolerance given an amount and ToleranceMode

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Empty
member.summary: Returns an empty Tolerance object, equivalent to specifying no tolerance. In most cases, it results in an exact match but for floats and doubles a default tolerance may be used.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Zero
member.summary: Returns a zero Tolerance object, equivalent to specifying an exact match.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Mode
member.summary: Gets the ToleranceMode for the current Tolerance

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Value
member.summary: Gets the value of the current Tolerance instance.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Percent
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance, using the current amount as a percentage.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Ulps
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance, using the current amount in Ulps.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Days
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance with a TimeSpan as the amount, using the current amount as a number of days.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Hours
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance with a TimeSpan as the amount, using the current amount as a number of hours.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Minutes
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance with a TimeSpan as the amount, using the current amount as a number of minutes.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Seconds
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance with a TimeSpan as the amount, using the current amount as a number of seconds.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Milliseconds
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance with a TimeSpan as the amount, using the current amount as a number of milliseconds.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.Ticks
member.summary: Returns a new tolerance with a TimeSpan as the amount, using the current amount as a number of clock ticks.

member.name: P:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.IsEmpty
member.summary: Returns true if the current tolerance is empty.

member.name: M:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.Tolerance.CheckLinearAndNumeric
member.summary: Tests that the current Tolerance is linear with a numeric value, throwing an exception if it is not.

member.name: T:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.ToleranceMode
member.summary: Modes in which the tolerance value for a comparison can be interpreted.

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.ToleranceMode.None
member.summary: The tolerance was created with a value, without specifying how the value would be used. This is used to prevent setting the mode more than once and is generally changed to Linear upon execution of the test.

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.ToleranceMode.Linear
member.summary: The tolerance is used as a numeric range within which two compared values are considered to be equal.

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.ToleranceMode.Percent
member.summary: Interprets the tolerance as the percentage by which the two compared values my deviate from each other.

member.name: F:NUnit.Framework.Constraints.ToleranceMode.Ulps
member.summary: Compares two values based in their distance in representable numbers.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.AllowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is allowed as an input even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DisallowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is disallowed as an input even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the output will be non-null if the named parameter is non-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the associated parameter name.
member.param: The associated parameter name. The output will be non-null if the argument to the parameter specified is non-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the associated parameter name.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnAttribute
member.summary: Applied to a method that will never return under any circumstance.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method will not return if the associated Boolean parameter is passed the specified value.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified parameter value.
member.param: The condition parameter value. Code after the method will be considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.ParameterValue
member.summary: Gets the condition parameter value.
